pairs.question: When was John Donald McPherson born and when did he die?
pairs.answer: John Donald McPherson was born on February 20, 1945, and died on November 24, 2001.

pairs.question: At what age did John Donald McPherson win the 1963 World Figure Skating Championships, and why was this significant?
pairs.answer: McPherson won the 1963 World Figure Skating Championships at the age of 18, making him the youngest men's World Champion in history.

pairs.question: What notable achievements did John Donald McPherson accomplish in the 1960 Winter Olympics?
pairs.answer: He represented Canada at the 1960 Winter Olympics and placed 10th, becoming the youngest competitor at the age of 15. He turned 15 during the Olympic figure skating competition.

pairs.question: What unusual equipment did John Donald McPherson use to win the 1963 World Figure Skating Championships?
pairs.answer: He won the championship using an unusual pair of skates with small serrations in the blades.

pairs.question: After retiring from amateur competition, what professional skating activities did John Donald McPherson participate in?
pairs.answer: After his amateur career, McPherson turned professional and starred in Dick Button's Ice-Travanganza at the 1964 New York World's Fair. He also toured for 11 years with Holiday on Ice and won the 1965 World Professional Figure Skating Championships.

pairs.question: Which halls of fame inducted John Donald McPherson, and in what years?
pairs.answer: He was inducted into the Canadian Sports Hall of Fame in 1963 and the Canadian Figure Skating Hall of Fame in 1996.

pairs.question: Where did John Donald McPherson spend the later years of his life, and what was the cause of his death?
pairs.answer: He moved to Munich, Germany, in his later years and died there on November 24, 2001, due to complications arising from diabetes.
